title: Measure deferred convergence hardening before adding complexity
goal: Evaluate the remaining review suggestions for connector-snapshot persistence, root-task backend routing and convergence evidence validation under reproducible concurrency, latency and failure tests, then implement only changes with demonstrated safety or operational benefit.

approval.note: Do not simplify defensive persistence or add rate limiting from intuition alone. Preserve compatibility and fail-closed behavior.

acceptance.assertion: Multi-process bind tests measure lock contention, duplicate identical binds, stale-release invalidation and receipt durability; idempotency or rate limits are added only when they preserve exact release and declaration binding.

For the same live receipt and runtime release, grabowski_status and grabowski_contract_drift either report the same connector observability state or explicitly expose and test their intentionally different claim boundaries.

Root and user backend routing is mapped across start, observe, status, logs, cancel, resume and reconcile paths; any abstraction proves behavior parity for pre-dispatch failure, outcome_unknown and terminal lease release.
Convergence classifications are represented by an explicit tested decision table covering all supported mixed signals, while invalid input remains distinct from unknown evidence.
CPU, JSON hashing and receipt costs are measured at maximum supported batch sizes before a session or owner rate limit is proposed; read-only use is not throttled without evidence.
Public tool count, existing grip receipts, stored connector receipts and task database compatibility remain unchanged or have an explicit migration contract.
Focused concurrent tests and full repository validation pass on the exact reviewed head, with performance results and rejected optimizations documented.
The task does not weaken no-follow, private-file, self-hash, outcome_unknown, lease, audit or non-authority contracts merely to reduce code size.

metadata.source: Deferred, non-critical findings from reviews of Grabowski PRs #256-#258 and the post-deploy live readback of PR #260.
reviewed_topics: connector bind idempotency and concurrent access
root backend routing abstraction and recovery tests
central evidence validation, classification table and abuse budget

live_projection_finding.status_claim: client snapshot observable, fresh and matched for 141 tools
live_projection_finding.severity: observability inconsistency; runtime tool contract itself matches

does_not_establish: that a class hierarchy is preferable
that the matched connector receipt is invalid
